personal.name: Manu
personal.bio: Backend engineer with 4+ years of proven delivery experience across APIs and production systems, including 2+ years leading independent product and open-source development. Specialized in .NET, ASP.NET Core, and Entity Framework Core with a strong focus on clean architecture.

introduction: I'm a backend developer with **4+ years of proven backend delivery experience** across product teams and client projects, including **2+ years of independent product development**.

My technical journey has evolved from working with different backend technologies to now specializing in the **.NET ecosystem**. I focus on building clean, maintainable APIs using ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework Core, and modern development practices.
I'm passionate about backend architecture, database design, and writing code that's easy for teams to work with. Currently expanding my expertise in cloud-native development with Azure.
about.goal: Open to backend .NET roles. APIs, architecture, systems. No frontend.

experience.title: Independent Software Developer
experience.narrative: Successfully transitioned to full-time independent development. Building production-grade .NET applications and maintaining impactful open-source projects. Proving the viability of indie development while contributing meaningfully to the .NET ecosystem.
responsibilities: 📦 Built and published RecurPixel.Notify — a 37-package .NET notification framework (1 core, 1 orchestrator, 30+ adapters) with 4,800+ total NuGet downloads
🏗️ Building **ShopCore** — enterprise-grade multi-vendor e-commerce backend with 150+ API endpoints, Clean Architecture, and CQRS
🔧 Architected production systems using ASP.NET Core 10, Entity Framework Core, advanced design patterns, and Azure
👨‍💻 Contributing to .NET community through open-source development and helping other developers
📚 Deepened expertise in cloud-native architecture, scalability patterns, and advanced .NET features

experience.title: Developer (Part-time, Strategic)
experience.narrative: Deliberately shifted to part-time consulting while launching independent projects. A strategic move — having proven expertise allowed returning on flexible terms to fund and develop personal ventures. This phase proved the foundation was strong enough to serve clients while building the future.
responsibilities: Served as experienced consultant on part-time basis for established client relationships
Maintained and optimized critical backend API systems for production applications
Managed cloud infrastructure (AWS EC2, Linux VPS) ensuring high availability and performance
Debugged and enhanced features across complex distributed systems
Leveraged proven track record to negotiate flexible arrangement supporting independent work
keyWins: Demonstrated ability to balance consulting with personal projects
Maintained strong client relationships while pursuing bigger goals

experience.title: Web Developer (Freelance)
experience.narrative: Took the leap into independence. Built diverse client projects while aggressively upskilling in .NET ecosystem. This period proved both capability and hunger — completing projects while mastering new technologies showed the versatility needed for success.
responsibilities: Built end-to-end solutions for startup and SME clients across multiple industries
Developed complete backend systems with database architectures designed from scratch
Intensively mastered .NET ecosystem (C#, ASP.NET Core, Entity Framework Core) through real-world application
Managed full project lifecycle: requirements → architecture → development → deployment → support
Delivered 5+ complete projects with 100% client satisfaction
keyWins: Proved ability to work independently with clients
Deep technical learning through diverse projects
Established foundation for sustainable independent work

experience.title: Developer (Full-time Foundation)
experience.narrative: Launched professional career fresh from M.Sc. Worked with experienced teams on real production systems, learning industry best practices, handling critical business logic, and building strong engineering fundamentals. This foundation made everything that came after possible.
responsibilities: Led backend development for 10+ client applications across different business domains
Designed RESTful APIs and database schemas for scalable systems
Integrated complex third-party services: payment gateways (Razorpay, Stripe), OAuth, SMS/email providers
Built comprehensive admin dashboards and business management platforms
Collaborated with senior developers and learned production engineering culture
Improved system performance through optimization and architectural improvements
keyLearnings: Production-grade code quality and testing practices
API design and database optimization principles
Payment processing and security best practices
Working in team environments and code reviews

technicalSkills.database: SQL Server, T-SQL, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Database Design

projects.name: RecurPixel.Notify
projects.description: A .NET notification framework — not a single package, but a full ecosystem. One core SDK, one orchestration layer, 30+ provider adapters, and 2 meta packages. Handles multi-channel notification delivery (Email, SMS, Push, WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, Teams, Mattermost, Rocket.Chat, Telegram, Facebook, LINE, Viber, In-App) through a single consistent interface. Zero infrastructure required — pure library, your keys, your delivery log.
highlights: 35 published NuGet packages — 1 core, 1 orchestrator, 30+ adapters, 2 meta packages
14+ notification channels through a single consistent interface
Zero infrastructure — pure library, no platform, no SaaS dependency
DI-native with ASP.NET Core integration
Retry with exponential backoff
Cross-channel fallback chains
Parallel dispatch support
Hook-based delivery logging

projects.name: ShopCore
projects.description: Dual-purpose backend combining a traditional multi-vendor e-commerce marketplace with a subscription-based recurring delivery system — two completely different business models powered by one backend. Built for the Indian market with support for daily delivery cycles, flexible billing, and location-based vendor discovery.
highlights: One backend powers two completely different business models — marketplace and subscription product
Flexible billing: weekly, monthly, or fixed-day cycles with daily delivery
Optional deposit system with automated settlement logic
Multi-item subscriptions in one plan (milk + newspaper + bread)
Vendor-led customer onboarding and location-based vendor discovery
Subscription lifecycle management: skip, pause, resume
Razorpay and Stripe integration with role-based access control
PDF invoice generation, email and in-app notifications
xUnit integration tests with WebApplicationFactory

projects.name: RecurPixel.EasyMessages
projects.description: Zero-configuration .NET message library. 100+ built-in standardized error, success, and validation messages with a fluent, IntelliSense-driven API. Drop it in and start returning consistent messages immediately.
highlights: 100+ built-in messages — Authentication, CRUD, Validation, System errors
Type-safe IntelliSense-driven API with compile-time safety
Multiple output formats — JSON, XML, Console, Plain Text
Zero configuration — works immediately out of the box
Fluent chainable API for clean, readable code
IOptions pattern with full configuration support and presets
Extensible — custom messages, formatters, and interceptors
High performance — optimized for .NET 5–10, minimal overhead

books.title: Book 1: C# Fundamentals
books.description: A practical C# foundation that moves from language basics to real-world concurrency and reflection so you can write confident production code.

books.title: Book 2: ASP.NET Core Development (Quick Reference Cards)
books.description: A fast, developer-first ASP.NET Core reference covering the full API lifecycle from project setup to testing, security, and diagnostics.

books.title: Book 3: Complete Workbook
books.description: A guided workbook that helps you study in the right order, practice intentionally, and prepare for interviews with less guesswork.

books.title: Book 4: Advanced Guides & References
books.description: An advanced engineering handbook for building scalable backend systems with stronger architecture, performance, and platform-level practices.

books.title: Book 5: Advanced Concepts (In Progress)
books.description: An upcoming deep-dive focused on modern backend engineering: microservices architecture, DevOps workflows, and system design for real production scale.

contact.message: I'm currently open to backend development opportunities, especially roles working with .NET and ASP.NET Core. Feel free to reach out for collaborations, questions, or just to connect!
